steps.title: Identify what must follow 'need'
steps.content: The blank appears after 'need' in 'they rest when they need ______ their food.' The structure shows a verb ('need') that requires a verbal complement before the direct object 'their food.'

steps.title: Apply the infinitive rule for 'need'
steps.content:
The verb need requires an infinitive complement. To digest is the infinitive form that correctly works here.

The bare infinitive digest lacks the to; will digest is a future tense; and digesting is a gerund — none of these are infinitives.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
